title: The Arms Act, 1959
full_text: The Arms Act, 1959 आयुध अधिनियम, 1959 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 195954 Act Number: 54 Enactment Date: 1959-12-23 Act Year: 1959 Short Title: The Arms Act, 1959 Hindi Title: आयुध अधिनियम, 1959 Long Title: An Act to consolidate and amend the law relating to arms and ammunition. Ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs Department: Department of Internal Security Enforcement Date: 01-10-1962 Notification: 1st October, 1962,vide notification No. G.S.R. 992, dated 13th July, 1962, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3(i). TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Arms Act, 1959 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II ACQUISITION, POSSESSION, MANUFACTURE, SALE, IMPORT, EXPORT AND TRANSPORT OF ARMS AND AMMUNITION CHAPTER III PROVISIONS RELATING TO LICENCES CHAPTER IV POWERS AND PROCEDURE CHAPTER V OFFENCES AND PENALTIES CHAPTER VI MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions and interpretation. Section 3. Licence for acquisition and possession of firearms and ammunition. Section 4. Licence for acquisition and possession of arms of specified description in certain cases. Section 5. Licence for manufacture, sale, etc., of arms and ammunition. Show Related Subordinates Section 6. Licence for the shortening of guns or conversion of imitation firearms into firearms. Section 7. Prohibition of acquisition or possession, or of manufacture or sale of prohibited arms or prohibited ammunition. Show Related Subordinates Section 8. Prohibition of sale or transfer of firearms not bearing identification marks. Section 9. Prohibition of acquisition or possession by, or of sale or transfer to, young persons and certain other persons of firearms, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 10. Licence for import and export of arms, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 11. Power to prohibit import or export of arms, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 12. Power to restrict or prohibit transport of arms. Show Related Subordinates Section 13. Grant of licences. Show Related Subordinates Section 14. Refusal of licences. Section 15. Duration and renewal of licence. Section 16. Fees, etc., for licence. Show Related Subordinates Section 17. Variation, suspension and revocation of licences. Show Related Subordinates Section 18. Appeals. Show Related Subordinates Section 19. Powers to demand production of licence, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 20. Arrest of persons conveying arms, etc., under suspicious circumstances. Section 21. Deposit of arms, etc., on possession ceasing to be lawful. Show Related Subordinates Section 22. Search and seizure by magistrate. Show Related Subordinates Section 23. Search of vessels, vehicles for arms, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 24. Seizure and detention under orders of the Central Government. Show Related Subordinates Section 24A. Prohibition as to possession of notified arms in disturbed areas, etc. Section 24B. Prohibition as to carrying of notified arms in or through public places in disturbed areas etc. Section 25. Punishment for certain offences. Section 26. Secret contraventions. Section 27. Punishment for using arms, etc. Section 28. Punishment for use and possession of firearms or imitation firearms in certain cases. Section 29. Punishment for knowingly purchasing arms, etc., from unlicensed person or for delivering arms, etc., to person not entitled to possess the same. Section 30. Punishment for contravention of licence or rule. Section 31. Punishment for subsequent offences. Section 32. Power to confiscate. Section 33. Offences by companies. Section 34. Sanction of Central Government for warehousing of arms. Section 35. Criminal responsibility of persons in occupation of premises in certain cases. Section 36. Information to be given regarding certain offences. Section 37. Arrest and searches. Section 38. Offences to be cognizable. Section 39. Previous sanction of the district magistrate necessary in certain cases. Section 40. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 41. Power to exempt. Show Related Subordinates Section 42. Power to take census of firearms. Section 43. Power to delegate. Show Related Subordinates Section 44. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 45. Act not to apply in certain cases. Section 46. Repeal of Act 11 of 1878.
